common.prefixInfoA1.ai_hint: First FAQ answer explaining what device prefixes and sub-accounts are. Contains inline <code> HTML tags.
common.prefixInfoA1.source_text: When you use the same seed phrase on multiple devices, they all share the same base cryptographic key (did:key:...). To prevent the devices from clashing (e.g. using the same transaction sequence numbers), each device needs a unique prefix (like laptop or phone). This splits the wallet into separate, safe branches.

common.prefixInfoA2.ai_hint: Second FAQ answer explaining how prefixes can be used for branding/recognition. Contains inline <code> HTML tags.
common.prefixInfoA2.source_text: Yes! The prefix is visible to your contacts (e.g., my-company:abc@did:key:...). Using a recognizable name (like your name or business name) makes it extremely easy for your partners to quickly identify and verify payments from you.

common.prefixInfoA3.ai_hint: Third FAQ answer explaining that device prefixes are optional and what happens if left blank. Contains <strong> HTML tags.
common.prefixInfoA3.source_text: Yes, it is optional. If you only use this wallet on this single device, leave the field empty. This creates a standard "Root Account". You do not lose any features or security.

common.prefixInfoA4.ai_hint: Fourth FAQ answer explaining that prefixes are permanent but new profiles can be created. Contains <strong> HTML tags.
common.prefixInfoA4.source_text: A prefix is permanently tied to a profile once it is created. However, you can always create another profile (sub-account) on the same device using the same seed phrase but with a different prefix. You do not need to delete your old profile to do this.

common.prefixInfoModalSubtitle.ai_hint: Subtitle text below the title in the device prefix informational modal.
common.prefixInfoModalSubtitle.source_text: Learn how to configure your multi-device sub-accounts safely.

common.prefixInfoModalTitle.ai_hint: Title of the informational modal about device prefixes and sub-accounts.

common.prefixInfoQ1.ai_hint: First FAQ question about what a device prefix / sub-account is. Begins with ❓ emoji.
common.prefixInfoQ1.source_text: ❓ What is a Device Prefix / Sub-Account?

common.prefixInfoQ2.ai_hint: Second FAQ question about using prefixes for branding/recognition. Begins with 🏢 emoji.
common.prefixInfoQ2.source_text: 🏢 Can I use it for branding / recognition?

common.prefixInfoQ3.ai_hint: Third FAQ question about whether prefix is optional and what happens when blank. Begins with ⚙️ emoji.
common.prefixInfoQ3.source_text: ⚙️ Is it optional? What if I leave it blank?

common.prefixInfoQ4.ai_hint: Fourth FAQ question about changing or setting the prefix later. Begins with ✏️ emoji.
common.prefixInfoQ4.source_text: ✏️ Can I change or set the prefix later?

common.prefixInfoSafety1.ai_hint: First safety alert paragraph: every device must use a different prefix. Contains <strong> HTML tags.
common.prefixInfoSafety1.source_text: Every active device using the same seed phrase must use a different prefix.

common.prefixInfoSafety2.ai_hint: Second safety alert paragraph explaining consequences of prefix collisions.
common.prefixInfoSafety2.source_text: Using the exact same prefix (including leaving it blank) on two devices at the same time will cause sequence collisions. This will result in severe errors and inevitably damage your reputation in the network.

common.prefixInfoSafetyTitle.ai_hint: Heading of the safety alert section in the prefix info modal.

common.prefixInfoUnderstandBtn.ai_hint: Button text to close the prefix info modal, confirming the user understands the explanation.
